recommended_overlap_notes: RISK pediatric ileal RNA-seq row. GSE57945 exposes CCFA_Risk_001-CCFA_Risk_322 sample identifiers, but GSE117993 uses accession-local rectal titles such as 1_RISK_Rectal_cCD and GSE93624 uses Sample1-Sample245 without CCFA_Risk IDs. Public GEO matrix/SOFT metadata do not provide a direct subject-ID crosswalk, so do not count DS005 as independent from DS040/DS041 or as overlapping with them without external subject mapping.
policy_note: For rows sharing a study_family but lacking public reconciled subject identifiers, keep count_as_independent_cohort=Unclear rather than assigning one TRUE parent and FALSE companions. TRUE/FALSE should be used only when public metadata prove independence, parent/component structure, or subject/sample duplication.

recommended_overlap_notes: RISK pediatric rectal RNA-seq row. GSE117993 contains 190 rectal biopsy samples titled 1_RISK_Rectal_cCD through 190_RISK_Rectal_UC, with diagnosis/location/subtype metadata but no CCFA_Risk-style subject IDs. Same RISK family as DS005 and DS041, but public GEO matrix/SOFT metadata do not prove exact subject overlap or independence. Do not sum as an independent RISK cohort without subject-level reconciliation.
policy_note: For RISK rows DS005, DS040, and DS041, public GEO metadata confirm the shared RISK family but expose incompatible accession-local identifiers; unique-participant counts require external or controlled subject-level mapping.

recommended_overlap_notes: RISK pediatric ileal Crohn RNA-seq row. GSE93624 contains 245 ileal samples titled Sample1-Sample245 and GEO states 210 treatment-naive pediatric Crohn disease patients plus 35 non-IBD controls from RISK. Same RISK family as DS005 and DS040, but public GEO matrix/SOFT metadata do not expose a direct subject-ID crosswalk. Do not count as independent from DS005/DS040 without external subject mapping.
policy_note: The absence of a public crosswalk is not evidence that the rows are independent. It only blocks de-duplication from public metadata, so Unclear is the safest registry value.
